name: Green Bowl / Green Bowls / Green Ball / Pantai Green Bowl
distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 18-20 km (35-45 min by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport) [uncertain]
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-90 min by scooter; From Uluwatu (Suluban/Single Fin): ~20-25 min by scooter
wave_type: Right-hand reef break (primary); a left reef peak that only works on spring low tides (full/new moon). Quick steep right peak over reef that walls up and barrels on the inside near the channel
skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (not suitable for beginners under any circumstances)
wave_height_range: Waist-high to 1.5x overhead; works from ~1 ft to 6 ft+; closes out beyond that
best_season: Wet season (Oct-Apr / Nov-Apr) for clean small swells with light winds; said to be 'mostly a rainy season surf spot'. Can also be surfed in dry season early mornings when other Bukit spots are too big
best_tide: Works on all tides (rare for the Bukit); mid tide is considered best; low tide for the rare left; outgoing tide creates very strong rip current
best_wind: Offshore N to NE winds (sheltered from the dominant SE trades that work for west-coast Bukit spots)
swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (picks up most south swells but very tide and size sensitive; often too big or current-affected)
wave_length_meters: approx. 40-100 m short punchy right; barrel section on the inside [uncertain]
wave_speed_rating: Fast (quick steep right peak, fast hollow wall, barrels inside)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: High - steep quick right peak over shallow reef; barrel section close to the channel; not forgiving on missed take-offs
secondary_peaks: Inside bowl/barrel section near the channel on the right; rare left peak that only breaks on spring low tides
peak_crowd_level: 2-3/5 (crowd factor cited 5/10 on good days; usually uncrowded thanks to the long stairs and demanding wave)
access: Walk down a long stone staircase carved into the cliff face; commonly cited as 'a few hundred' steps (varies 200-300+ in different sources). The trail passes through tropical forest with monkeys and traditional stone statues. Scooter parking is at the cliff-top
paddle_out_method: Paddle out through the channel on the right (north) side of the reef; do not paddle straight over the reef especially at lower tides
entry_fee: Parking ~IDR 2,000-5,000 (scooter) per day; beach entry free; small donation may be requested by parking attendants
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol to mid-morning during a rising mid tide to high; before the wind and current pick up
best_time_of_day: First light to ~10 am; avoid mid-afternoon when wind switches and outgoing tide rip strengthens
nearby_warung: Very limited - a couple of small warungs at the cliff top above the stairs; almost nothing at beach level apart from occasional coconut/snack vendors. Closest proper restaurants are 2+ km away in Ungasan
alternative_spot_within_10min: Nyang Nyang (~10-15 min north along the Bukit); Pandawa Beach (cruise/learners) nearby; Uluwatu/Padang Padang ~20 min if Green Bowl is too small or current-affected
hazards: One of the strongest currents on Bali (especially on outgoing tide), shallow live coral reef, freak/rogue sets, getting swept out the channel, slippery rocks and high-tide trail washouts, monkeys along trail, very limited rescue infrastructure
marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the reef, occasional jellyfish, rare tiger shark sightings reported; coral cuts common - flush with fresh water and disinfect, monitor for infection in tropical climate
reef_cut_severity_index: High - shallow live coral; reef boots strongly recommended, especially at low tide. Spare leash recommended because a broken leash plus the rip can strand you offshore
rip_current_pattern: Powerful rip running out through the channel at the end of the right-hander; greatly amplified on outgoing tide and bigger swells - can quickly take you well out to sea if you lose your board
lineup_etiquette: Standard reef-break etiquette - one wave per person, respect priority on the inside, no snaking, greet the regulars, do not paddle around to grab sets
localism_intensity: Low (cited 1/10) - relaxed lineup with a small regular crew; friendly if you respect the rotation
respect_protocol: Smile, say hi, do not litter, support local warungs/parking attendants, do not bring beginners into the lineup
resident_local_legends: No single iconic local surfer attached to Green Bowl in the public record; regular Bukit/Ungasan locals surf it [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Bukit Peninsula has cliff-side shrines and the Green Bowl area itself has small Hindu cave temples used for ceremonies - do not enter shrine caves with surf gear, dress modestly when off the beach; respect Nyepi silent day island-wide
medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (24h); BIMC Nusa Dua; Siloam Hospital Denpasar for serious injuries
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu approx. 7-9 km; BIMC Nusa Dua approx. 10-12 km [uncertain]
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue at this spot
emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; BASARNAS (Search & Rescue) 115; Unicare Clinic Pecatu +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
nearest_surf_school: Rapture Surfcamps Green Bowl / Padang Padang and Padang Padang Surf Camp operate guided sessions in the broader area; no school directly on the beach. Lessons here only offered to qualified intermediate+ guests
board_rental_availability: No board rental at the beach; rent in Uluwatu / Padang Padang / Ungasan before driving down
surf_photographer_available: No regular land/water photographer stationed at the lineup
photographer_recommendation: Freelance Bukit photographers can be hired by appointment via Instagram; rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Cari Surf Camp Pecatu, Rapture Surfcamps Green Bowl / Padang, Six Senses Uluwatu (luxury), Bvlgari Resort Bali (luxury, very close in Ungasan), various Ungasan/Pecatu villas and homestays within 5-10 min drive
drone_allowed: Generally allowed; the cliffs and bowl-shaped caves are a popular drone subject. Avoid flying over the small cliff-side temples and respect other beachgoers [uncertain]
coworking_nearby: No coworking nearby; closest in Bingin/Uluwatu (cafe-style) and Canggu (Tropical Nomad, Outpost, B Work) ~1+ hr away
water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season due to remote location and low development; can carry sediment after heavy wet-season rain; cleaner than west/north Bali beaches [uncertain]
